last_result: Cycle 36 complete. SI #28: Criteria Informativeness Asymmetry (CIA) — information-theoretic formalization of SI #24's qualitative claim G(I-criteria) >> G(B-criteria). Core result: MI(R; C | ¬RTO) measures the optimization gain in bits conferred to a non-RTO system by publishing criteria. B-channel: low MI (computational equivalence makes criteria useless for constraint-solving + test-detection difficulty prevents opportunistic application). I-channel: high MI (template exploitability reduces search to lookup + test-detection ease enables opportunistic application). CIA theorem: MI(C_B | ¬RTO) << MI(C_I | ¬RTO). CIA-General principle: assessment corrosiveness ∝ MI(C | ¬target) — computationally-equivalent + test-detection-resistant assessments safe to publish; template-exploitable + test-detection-susceptible assessments must restrict. Connection to SI #27: battery structure raises H(R_battery | C, ¬RTO) via CPP-collective, quantifying SI #27's mechanism in CIA terms. D4 streak: 31.
next_step: C37 candidates: (1) Joint B+I assessment protocol design (Priority A, carried from C35/C36) — how do B-channel scenarios (C31 template) and I-RTO battery (C35) compose into a full integrated assessment? Includes sequencing, score integration, diagnostic quadrant design. (2) CIA-General applied — can I-channel test-detection probability be reduced via naturalistic probe integration? (3) Long-horizon C50 items.
